outcome_text: Appropriateness of AI metrics and effectiveness of existing measures are regularly assessed and updated, including reports of errors and impacts on affected communities.
scoring_text: Ability to evaluate and update AI measurement effectiveness, including assessing metric appropriateness over time, incorporating error reports and community impact data into measurement refinement, and maintaining measurement system currency as AI systems and their impacts evolve.
_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Ability to evaluate and update AI measurement effectiveness, including assessing metric appropriateness over time, incorporating error reports and community impact data into measurement refinement, and maintaining measurement system currency as AI systems and their impacts evolve.
reference_document_element_description: Conduct AI impact assessments for new and materially modified AI systems, producing documented risk findings with recommended mitigations and residual risk statements.
comments: NIST AI RMF MS-1.2 (MEASURE) superset of WIDAI AG domain. AG-T-008 is the strongest match (STS: 0.631, cosine: 0.635). Secondary: AG-S-006 (AG, cosine: 0.625), AG-T-010 (AG, cosine: 0.625).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression. Primary method for all elements. Scores the competency_implication text (workforce competency interpretation of the risk management outcome) against WIDAI KSA statements.
primary_method.significance: STS 6.3/10 (raw 0.631). Shared vocabulary: [ai, impact, systems]. NIST AI RMF competency implication terms [ability, appropriateness, assessing, community, currency, data] vs WIDAI terms [assessments, conduct, documented, findings, materially, mitigations] define the semantic distance.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.855 (P=0.854, R=0.856). Balanced P/R — the 36-word competency implication and 22-word WIDAI KSA cover each other's token-level semantic content symmetrically.

nli_cross_encoder.description: Natural Language Inference classification. NLI entailment is systematically low for competency→competency pairs because NLI measures strict logical implication, not semantic relatedness. Primary diagnostic value is in detecting contradiction.
nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (100.0%). Standard result for cross-framework competency mapping — the NIST AI RMF element and WIDAI KSA describe overlapping competencies without strict logical implication.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.635 ≈ STS 0.631 (gap 0.004). Independent encoding and cross-attention agree. Shared terms [ai, impact, systems] are sufficient for independent encoding to detect the match.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 8.9%. Shared tokens: [ai, impact, systems]. NIST AI RMF-only: [ability, appropriateness, assessing, community, currency, data]. WIDAI-only: [assessments, conduct, documented, findings, materially, mitigations].
